turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Good evening, Streamline Cars, Karen speaking. How can I help?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Hi, I'd like to book a taxi to the airport, please, for early tomorrow morning.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Certainly. Let me take the details. Could I have your name first?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Yes, it's Raj Patel.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: And could you spell the surname for me?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Of course. It's P-A-T-E-L. Patel.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Thank you. And where would you like to be picked up?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: From nineteen Chestnut Avenue — sorry, no, nineteen Chestnut Close. It's easy to mix them up. Chestnut Close.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Not a problem — nineteen Chestnut Close. And which airport are you heading to?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Gatwick, please. My flight's at nine in the morning, so I'll need to leave nice and early.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: For a nine o'clock flight from Gatwick, I'd recommend leaving at half past five in the morning to be safe — the roads can be busy. Does that suit?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Half past five, yes, let's do that.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Great. And how many passengers, and how much luggage?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Just me, and two large suitcases plus a rucksack.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: That's fine, we'll send an estate car so there's plenty of room. The fare to Gatwick is a fixed price of fifty-five pounds. You can pay the driver by card or cash.

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: Fifty-five pounds, that's fine. Can I pay by card?

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Yes, all our cars take card. Now, could I take a mobile number, so the driver can let you know he's outside?

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: It's oh-seven-six-three-two, five-five-one, four-nine-oh.

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Let me repeat that — oh-seven-six-three-two, five-five-one, four-nine-oh. Perfect. Your booking reference is T, then three-zero-seven-four, so T3074. The driver will text you when he arrives.

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: T3074. And will he wait if I'm a couple of minutes late coming out?

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Of course, he'll wait. But do try to be ready, as it's a long drive. One tip — please double-check you've got your passport before you leave, we get so many people who forget it. Have a good trip, Raj.

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: I definitely won't forget the passport this time. One more thing — will I be able to book a car for the return journey too, when I fly back next week?

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: Absolutely. Just give us a ring the day before with your flight details and we'll pick you up from the airport. It's the same fixed price back into town, and the driver will be waiting in the arrivals hall with a sign showing your name.

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: That's very reassuring, thank you. And roughly how long does the drive to Gatwick take at that time of the morning?

turns.speaker: Karen
turns.text: At half past five the roads are usually quiet, so you're looking at around an hour and a quarter. That gives you plenty of time to check in and have a relaxed coffee before your flight.

turns.speaker: Raj
turns.text: That's perfect. Thank you very much for all your help, Karen. Good night.

Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.prompt_text: Before leaving, Raj is reminded to check he has his ______.

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: How will Raj pay for the taxi?
